title: Space Debris
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Space debris, also known (0) as space junk, consists of defunct satellites, spent rocket stages, and fragments from collisions orbiting Earth. This growing problem threatens both operational satellites and future space exploration.||Since the beginning of the space age in 1957, humans have launched thousands (9) of objects into orbit. While many have returned to Earth, burning up in the atmosphere, a significant amount remains in space. Scientists estimate there (10) are over 34,000 pieces of debris larger than 10 centimetres currently orbiting Earth, along with millions of smaller fragments that are (11) too small to track but still dangerous.||The main danger is (12) that space debris travels at incredibly high speeds – up to 28,000 kilometres per hour. At these velocities, even a tiny paint chip can damage a spacecraft, (13) while a collision with a larger object could be catastrophic. In 2009, an inactive Russian satellite collided with an American communications satellite, creating thousands of new debris fragments.||This problem could potentially trigger (14) a cascade effect known as Kessler Syndrome, where collisions create more debris, leading to more collisions, eventually making certain orbital regions unusable. Various solutions are (15) being developed, including debris removal systems using nets, harpoons, or lasers. However, the technology is expensive and complex. International cooperation will be essential (16) to address this challenge, as space debris affects all nations involved in space activities, regardless of who created it.

example.text: Space debris, also known (0) space junk, consists of defunct satellites.

questions.explanation: Expression 'thousands of objects' indicates a large number of objects.
Estructura 'there are' para indicar existencia con sujeto plural.
Estructura 'too small to track' indica exceso que impide algo.
Conjunction 'that' introduces a noun clause after 'is'.
Conjunction 'while' introduces contrast between different object sizes.
Present continuous passive: 'are being developed' indicates current development.
Structure 'essential to address' with a purpose infinitive.
